name: Auckland
intro: New Zealand's largest city and economic hub, Auckland has the most tradies and the most competitive market. Expect higher hourly rates reflecting the city's higher cost of living.

name: Wellington
intro: The nation's capital has a compact, hilly geography that makes some jobs more complex and costly. Strong demand from the public sector and growing residential market keeps tradies busy year-round.

name: Christchurch
intro: Canterbury's largest city has seen significant rebuild activity since the 2011 earthquake, creating a large and experienced tradie workforce. Rates are generally lower than Auckland and Wellington.

name: Hamilton
intro: The Waikato's main city is a fast-growing hub with strong residential construction demand. Rates are moderate and the tradie pool is growing to meet demand.

name: Tauranga
intro: One of NZ's fastest-growing cities, Tauranga has a booming construction sector driven by retirees and migration from Auckland. Demand is high and good tradies book out weeks in advance.

name: Dunedin
intro: Otago's main city has a mix of older character homes and new developments. The student population creates year-round demand for maintenance work. Rates are among the most affordable in NZ.

name: Palmerston North
intro: Palmy is a regional centre with a strong agricultural and education economy. Affordable housing prices drive renovation activity and tradie rates are moderate.

name: Napier
intro: Hawke's Bay's coastal city has strong demand for renovation work on its distinctive Art Deco housing stock. The growing wine and tourism industry also supports commercial construction.

name: Hastings
intro: Hastings serves the Hawke's Bay horticultural region and shares many tradies with nearby Napier. Strong demand for new residential builds and commercial work.

name: New Plymouth
intro: Taranaki's main city benefits from oil and gas industry money, supporting a well-paid workforce and strong demand for quality residential work. A good supply of experienced tradies.

name: Rotorua
intro: Rotorua's tourism economy drives demand for hospitality fit-outs and ongoing maintenance. The geothermal environment can affect some building materials, so local experience matters.

name: Whangārei
intro: Northland's main city has a mix of lifestyle blocks and urban residential demand. Growth from Aucklanders moving north is pushing construction activity up significantly.

name: Nelson
intro: Nelson's sunny climate and lifestyle appeal attract retirees and lifestyle block buyers, creating steady renovation and new build demand. A strong arts and creative community also drives unique fitout work.

name: Queenstown
intro: NZ's adventure tourism capital has some of the highest construction costs in the country due to remote location, high land values, and labour shortages. Expect a premium for all trade work.

name: Invercargill
intro: Southland's main city has the most affordable housing in NZ, creating a steady market for renovations and upgrades. Tradie rates are among the lowest in the country.

name: Lower Hutt
intro: Part of the Wellington metro area, Lower Hutt has strong residential demand and benefits from Wellington's busy construction sector. Rates align with greater Wellington.

name: Upper Hutt
intro: Upper Hutt is a suburban Wellington city with a mix of older homes ripe for renovation and new subdivisions. Good access to Wellington tradies.

name: Porirua
intro: A growing city north of Wellington with significant social housing renewal activity and strong residential construction demand.

name: Gisborne
intro: The first city to see the sun each day has a small but tight-knit tradie community. Forestry and agriculture drive commercial construction, while a growing lifestyle block market supports residential work.

name: Whanganui
intro: Whanganui's affordable property prices are attracting investors and first-home buyers who need renovation work. An up-and-coming river city with a growing arts and lifestyle scene.
